title: Tax Graduate
description: **Early Careers Academy \- Graduate Opportunities** --------------------------------------------------- You’ll be supported from day one, learning from experienced colleagues while gaining hands\-on experience. ### **What you'll need** * Right to work in Ireland from your start date. * Hold an Undergraduate degree in finance, accounting, or a related discipline. * Be on track to achieve or have already achieved a minimum 2:1 in your undergraduate degree. * Achieve a minimum of 450 points in your Leaving Cert or equivalent. **Why Azets Early Careers?** ---------------------------- * **Learning Opportunities:** First\-class training plus support to complete your qualification, study days, mentoring, and buddy support. * **Supportive Network:** Managers and peer network creating a collaborative, encouraging environment. * **Fun \& Dynamic Culture:** Social engagement, community involvement, and exciting work experiences. **The Azets Academy: Role Overview** ------------------------------------ We are proud to introduce the Azets Academy: a market\-leading 3\.5\-year graduate training programme, designed to help you build the skills, confidence, and experience needed to become a fully qualified accountant. From day one, you’ll be supported by experts, exposed to real client work, and given the platform to grow both personally and professionally in a fast\-moving, collaborative environment. ### **Key Highlights** * Direct access to Partners and senior leadership, offering unrivalled learning and mentoring opportunities. * International travel opportunities, allowing you to build your network and gain exposure across our European offices. ### **Your Role as an Associate Graduate Trainee** As an Associate Graduate Trainee, you’ll join a highly experienced and supportive team, gaining hands\-on exposure across audit, tax, and accounting. You’ll be encouraged to broaden your experience, take ownership of your development, and continuously build your technical and professional skillset — all with full support throughout your journey. **Training Programme** You’ll complete a structured 3\.5\-year training pathway designed to support your journey to becoming a fully qualified accountant, combining professional study with hands\-on, real\-world experience. **Inclusion \& Accessibility** ------------------------------ We are proud to be a **Mindful Employer** and **Disability Confident Employer**. Creating an inclusive workplace isn’t just a statement – it’s something we live every day. At Azets, we value **diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences**. Even if your experience doesn’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you – you might be a great fit for another opportunity. **earlycareersrecruitment@azets.co.uk** **Our Benefits for Early Careers** ---------------------------------- Everything you need to grow your skills, feel supported, and enjoy life while you train. ### **Study Support \& Exams** Fully funded qualifications, paid study leave and exam fees, plus mentoring and a dedicated buddy to support you through training. ### **Wellbeing \& Support** 24/7 GP access, mental health support, Employee Assistance Programme, and a friendly, inclusive workplace culture that puts people first. ### **Life Outside Work** Flexible and hybrid working, time off on your birthday, holiday purchase scheme, discounts and perks platform, and community volunteering days. ### **Career Progression** Clear career pathways, structured training plans, mentoring from experienced colleagues, and real responsibility from day one. **Move forward with confidence.** ================================= At Azets Ireland, our purpose is to improve the lives of our clients, colleagues and communities, in a sustainable way. ### **4,000\+ Colleagues** A people\-powered business across Ireland, the UK and Europe. ### **70\+ Offices** Locally embedded across Ireland and the UK, with international reach. ### **Leading Accountancy Firm** Bringing deep local expertise backed by the scale of the Azets Group. ### **80,000\+ Clients** From local entrepreneurs to international groups. Curious about life at Azets Ireland? Meet our people across the Azets Group. Job Reference: IE00084

title: Team Member
description: **SHIFTS YOU ARE APPLYING FOR:** **5\.25hrs p/w; Fri 15:30 \- 21:00** Working as part of a fast\-paced store team, your top priority will be to offer outstanding service and excellent stock operations to our customers. In return for supporting all Company policies and procedures and having a great attitude to work, we will invest in you and your development from day one. Flexible working options are available. **About the Role:** To be a successful Sales Team Member you will: * Offer fast and friendly service at all times, helping our customers to find the perfect products * Share your passion and knowledge about our amazing products * Work in all areas of the store, including sales floors, stockrooms and processing deliveries * Take control of your own development We'll offer amazing benefits (see further list below) **About You:** * A great communicator who’s always looking for ways you can help * Friendly, calm and efficient – even on your busiest days * Excited about the challenge of a varied and fast\-paced job * Flexible, supportive and always ready to go the extra mile **What’s Next** As part of your application you will be required to complete an online assessment. This will involve a Working with Numbers Assessment and a Retail Scenarios Assessment. Our in\-store recruitment team will then review the successfully completed applications and those that match the job criteria closest will be contacted to arrange an initial Interview so we can have a chat to find out more about you. In order to apply for this position you must not have had an unsuccessful online assessment (as detailed above) in the last 6 months. Best of luck! \#Assistant \#Customer \#Shop \#Associate \#Consultant \#Delivery \#Colleague * Recognition and rewards for doing a great job and achieving great results * 25% off most NEXT, MADE\*, Lipsy\*, Gap\* and Victoria's Secret\* products (\*when purchased through NEXT) * 10% off most partner brands \& up to 15% off Branded Beauty * 60% off a generous (optional) working wardrobe allowance to buy clothes to wear for work * Early VIP access to sale stock * Free health and wellbeing services * Life assurance * You can register for a discounted health plan for you and your family Support Networks \- Access to Network Groups to empower and celebrate each other * Conditions apply to all benefits. These benefits are discretionary and subject to change. We aim to support all candidates during the application process and are happy to provide workplace adjustments when necessary. Should you need support with your application due to a disability or long\-term condition, feel free to get in touch with us by email careers@next.co.uk (please include 'Workplace Adjustments' in the subject line), or call us on 0116 479 2223 / 0044 116 479 2223 (line opening times are Monday to Thursday 9am \- 5pm; Friday 9am \- 4\.45pm; Saturday 9am \- 5pm \& Sunday 9am \- 4pm. Excludes bank holidays). You know Next, but did you know we’re a FTSE\-100 retail company employing over 35,000 people across the UK and Ireland. We’re the UK’s 2nd largest fashion retailer and for Kidswear we’re the market leader. At the last count we have over 500 stores, plus the Next Online and it’s now possible to buy on\-line from over 70 countries around the world! So we’ve gone global!

title: Trade Settlement Analyst
description: **Job Title:** Trade Settlement Analyst **Location:** Dublin (5x days on\-site per week) **Salary/Rate:** €250\-300 per day **Start Date:** April **Job Type:** Initial 6 month contract **Company Introduction** We have an exciting opportunity now available with one of our sector\-leading consultancy clients! They are currently looking for a skilled Trade Settlement Analyst to join their asset management client in Dublin. **Job Responsibilities/Objectives** The Fund \& Life Operations team is responsible for Post\-Execution Trade Management across multiple Asset Classes Equity, Fixed Income, FX, Money Market, Exchange Traded and OTC Derivatives. The team is responsible for several regulatory reporting tasks and acts as point of contact for queries relating to Trade Events, Valuation, Documentation, Reporting and Reconciliations issue resolution. The successful candidate will ensure timely trade settlement and failing trade process as well as account set ups. • The successful candidate will have the responsibility for ensuring timely trade settlement across multiple asset types (Equity, Fixed Income and FXs) • Processing and resolving failed trades as well as CSDR penalties • Perform data reporting and analysis to assist in the development of business, financial and regulatory reports as well as general data analysis to support change and Management reporting. • Identifying areas for improvements and implement changes where necessary in relation to industry standards, commercial viability, and ensuring the changes are fit for purpose. **Required Skills/Experience** The ideal candidate will have the following: • The candidate will have relevant experience combined with a background in middle or back office within an asset or fund manager. • Knowledge and some experience in dealing with the following asset types: Equities, ETFs, Fixed Income, FX and Exchange Traded Derivatives will be a distinct advantage. • Smart, hardworking and highly collaborative individual with the ability to efficiently interact with all levels within the company (from entry level trainees through to senior managers). • A systematic approach to problem solving; ability to analyse problems and trace them to their root causes • Organised individual who can self\-manage multiple priorities, routine work and urgent issues as they arise. The ideal candidate is focused on resolving issues quickly. • Appropriate 3rd Level Qualification.

title: Trainee Data Technician
description: ONNEC Group are a leading independent technology partner and global integrator, with over 30 years experience, and a 800\+ team of global experts, specialising in providing end\-to\-end connectivity solutions that propel organisations everywhere. From structured cabling to managed services, our end\-to\-end services give infrastructure that can be completely replied on. Design. Build. Deploy. Optimise. We offer a complete solution for business connectivity. We are a rapidly growing organisation and finding and retaining the highest calibre of people is fundamental to us for the success of our business, we work hard to create a challenging, supportive and satisfying working environment for all our employees. ONNEC has successfully achieved the Investors in Diversity Foundational Award for our commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion in the workplace. Due to the growing demands of our clients, we have a requirement for an enthusiastic and motivated individual to join our team as a Trainee Structured Cabling / Data Technician, who will be based in Dublin. This is an excellent opportunity to work with Ireland’s largest IT network infrastructure solutions company and be part of some exciting projects including large multinational companies. Previous experience will be advantageous, however, training will be provided. **What you’ll be doing as our Trainee Data Technician:** * Wiring of copper Cat6, Cat6A, Fibre and Voice cable * Terminating \& testing of copper cable * Pulling cables (Cat 6, Cat 6A, Copper Cable, Fibre Optic Multicore) * Splicing \& testing of fibre cable * Basic cable fault finding * Reading \& marking of site drawings * Patching and circuits * Health \& Safety procedures on construction sites **What we’re looking for in our Trainee Data Technician:** * Excellent communication and time keeping skills * Strong work ethic and willingness to learn * Flexible to travel when needed * Valid Safepass * Own transport desireable If you feel you have the required skills and experience, click **apply** now to be considered as our **Trainee Data Technician** – we’d love to hear from you!

title: Twilight Veterinary Surgeon - OOH / Emergency
description: **Twilight Veterinary Practitioner – OOH / Emergency** ------------------------------------------------------ **All Pets Veterinary Hospital, Drogheda, Co Louth** **Part Time, Permanent** **Salary up to €70,000 (FTE) DOE plus excellent benefits** ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- **Be Part of Something New** ---------------------------- This is a rare opportunity to join a brand new ECC and out\-of\-hours service from the very beginning. All Pets Veterinary Hospital is already highly regarded across Ireland for its clinical standards and collaborative team, and this new service is being built to set a new benchmark for emergency care in the region. You’ll be working as part of a dedicated night team of experienced professionals, in an environment designed to deliver high\-quality, consistent emergency care while being fully supported by a well\-established hospital. **A Twilight Role That Actually Supports Work\-Life Balance** ------------------------------------------------------------- You’ll work every Sunday \+ bank holidays with shifts running from 2:30pm to 7:30pm when the night team will take over. **Work at the Cutting Edge of Emergency Care** ---------------------------------------------- Based in the Drogheda hospital, you’ll have access to excellent facilities including digital radiography, ultrasound, a fully equipped in\-house lab, modern surgical theatres, and dedicated hospital wards. While CT and MRI are not on\-site, the team works closely with UCD to ensure fast\-tracked referrals when needed. This is a busy, established hospital, so your caseload will be varied, interesting, and genuinely rewarding \- ideal for someone looking to build depth and confidence in emergency medicine. **A Strong, Supportive Team** ----------------------------- You’ll be joining a large, experienced, and approachable team, with a range of clinical interests across surgery, medicine, dentistry, and ECC. It’s a collaborative environment where people support each other, share knowledge, and take pride in delivering excellent patient care. Development is actively encouraged, with funded CPD, support for further qualifications, and paid professional memberships. **Who This Role Would Suit** ---------------------------- This role is ideal for a confident, experienced vet who enjoys emergency work and is comfortable working independently overnight. You would need to be competent in small animal medical and surgical emergencies. **You must be VCI registered, and while ECC experience is an advantage, a strong interest and willingness to continue developing in this area is just as important. Due to the nature of the role, it’s not suited to new graduates or those requiring close day\-to\-day mentorship.** **Benefits \- Subject to Pro Rata** ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- We recognise that our people are fundamental to the success of our business. Investing in our staff, premises and processes is at the heart of what we do. In addition to a competitive salary, you will also benefit from: ### **Work Life Balance** * Enhanced Annual Leave entitlement * Birthday Leave (paid day off on your birthday) ### **Focus on Employee Wellbeing** * Paid Maternity Leave Package * Paid Paternity Leave * Cycle to Work Scheme * Full access to EAP and Wellbeing Centre * Paid Bereavement Leave * Company Sick Pay ### **Development** * €1300 annual CPD allowance with 5 days paid CPD days pro rata * Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Career Progression Opportunities, both clinical and non\-clinical ### **Professional Memberships** * VCI membership paid * VDS cover paid ### **Additional Benefits** * Annual Pay Reviews * Dedicated CARE fund – annual contribution of €1,100 from IVC Evidensia for each Vet to use on a case of your choice to ensure successful outcome with your patient (T\&Cs apply) * Pawesome Benefits \- reward gateway giving discounts and cashback with hundreds of retailers * Employer Pension Contributions * Free Flu Inoculations * Discounted Veterinary Fees * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQ\+, Wellbeing Champions, Clinical Board and many more) A Career That Grows With You This isn’t just another night role. It’s an opportunity to be part of building something new, within a hospital that already has a strong reputation for excellence. If you’re looking for a role where you can develop your emergency skillset, work with a great team, and enjoy a rota that genuinely gives you your time back, we’d love to hear from you. Salary up to €70000 (FTE) DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Vendor Analyst
description: **Calling all innovators – find your future at Fiserv.** We’re Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day – quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. **Job Title** Vendor Analyst **What does a successful Payments Vendor Analyst do?** A successful Vendor Analyst at Fiserv shapes and delivers payment solutions that let our clients meet their customers where they are, so people can focus on life—not payments. You will blend market insight, technical understanding and operational excellence to launch secure, compliant and user\-friendly card\-present and card\-not\-present products. You will collaborate across teams to drive continuous improvement and ensure exceptional merchant and client outcomes. **What You Will Do** * Own and drive the product vision and roadmap for card\-present and card\-not\-present payment solutions and related services. * Monitor market trends, regulatory changes and competitor activity to identify product opportunities and risks. * Lead end\-to\-end operational readiness for product launches and enhancements, ensuring processes, playbooks and SLAs are in place. * Collaborate with Operations, Risk, Compliance, Client Experience and external vendors to align requirements, training and support models. * Ensure regulatory and scheme compliance, partnering with Compliance and Risk to manage audits and certifications. * Monitor product performance and operational KPIs, and drive optimisation initiatives to improve transaction success and merchant experience. * Communicate product updates, operational impacts and launch status to stakeholders across the business and with external partners. **What You Will Need To Have** * Deep knowledge of payments, acquiring and merchant services, with practical understanding of card\-present and card\-not\-present flows. * Technical familiarity with POS architecture, payment gateways, terminal management systems, EMV and contactless technologies. * Proven capability in operational readiness, including process design, incident management and service delivery frameworks. * Strong analytical skills to interpret performance data and convert insights into measurable product and operational improvements. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills with strong organisational and project management capability. **What Would Be Great To Have** * Awareness of PCI DSS controls and payment scheme rules (Visa, Mastercard, etc.). * Familiarity with Irish and EU payment regulations and compliance expectations. * Experience working with third‑party title: Wholesale / Showroom Intern – Dublin
description: ABOUT MAX MARA FASHION GROUP Max Mara Fashion Group is recognised throughout the world as the precursor of modern pret\-à\-porter fashion. The Company was founded in 1951 with the aim of offering “haute de gamme” feminine clothing, produced according to excellent industrial processes. Passion, commitment and tradition have created a unique fashion style. **Job Description: Wholesale Showroom Intern – Dublin (15th to 19th June)** We are currently seeking an intern for the MaxMara wholesale team for 1 week in our Dublin showroom. This is an exciting opportunity for any students looking to explore different roles in the luxury fashion industry. **Main Responsibilities:** Sample Management: * Unpacking, inventorying and merchandising samples for new collections * Ensuring samples are organised and stored in perfect condition at all times Showroom Support: * Support setting up the showrooms for the selling campaign alongside the VM team * Ensure visual merchandising standards are always adhered to across all brands * Managing refreshments for appointments during the selling campaign Operational Activities: * Assisting in appointments where required with sales managers * Support sales manager with order entry during campaign * Order checking with the sales managers and logistics team **Ideal Candidate:** Key Technical Skills: * Strong planning and organisational skills * Strong verbal and written communication skills * Excellent interpersonal skills, with a strong ability to successfully and confidently interact with all stakeholders, at all levels Key Behavioural Skills: * Highly organised and able to prioritise, plan and manage a demanding and ever\-changing workload * Keen attention to detail and accuracy * Good team player and comfortable working on their own * Proactive and problem solver mind\-set and approach * Adept at learning and developing * Friendly and approachable manner We offer a daily lunch allowance for all members of our staff.

title: Windows Server Administrator
description: **Windows System Administrator \- 3rd Level Support Level** **Job Description:** Our client is one of the top clients to work for in Ireland. The client employs over 4000 staff in Ireland and have multiple sites throughout Ireland. You will be part of their existing System infrastructure team internal IT team. This is a hybrid\-based position with excellent opportunities to contribute to the enhancement and stability of enterprise\-scale virtualisation and automation infrastructure **Job Responsibilities:** * Design, deploy, and maintain virtualisation platforms (such as **Hyper\-V** ) to ensure scalability, availability, and secure system environments. * Develop and manage automated scripts and workflows using PowerShell and/or Python to optimise virtual infrastructure management tasks. * **Provide mainly 3rd level support** for both on\-site and remote server infrastructure, handling troubleshooting and system recovery when needed. * Implement system upgrades and integration of hardware/software solutions with minimal impact on operational continuity. * Document technical procedures, architectural designs, and operational support guides for internal use. * Collaborate with stakeholders to align infrastructure with organisational and security objectives. * Oversee incident management, including escalation processes to internal teams or external suppliers. * Act as a subject matter expert in virtualisation and automation, mentoring junior staff and promoting best practices **Experience Required:** * Minimum 10 years of experience working in Windows Server Engineering and Virtualization. * Strong scripting and automation capabilities, particularly using PowerShell. * Solid understanding of Microsoft networking components including Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, and DHCP. * Proven ability to lead technical projects from design through to implementation and support. * Familiarity with multi\-cloud and hybrid environments including platforms such as Azure, AWS, and Google Cloud * **Valid driver’s license (Must have)** **Desirable Skills:** * Experience with integrating virtualisation platforms with storage technologies such as NetApp, SAN, or NAS. * Exposure to infrastructure\-as\-code tools and methodologies. * Working knowledge of Linux/Unix systems. * Experience with Cisco networking solutions. * Familiarity with disaster recovery planning and implementation. **Educational Requirements:** * A third\-level qualification in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field is preferred **How to Apply:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV
